title: Thelymitra antennifera
taxonomy: Thelymitra antennifera is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Thelymitra. This genus is part of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as orchids. Thelymitra antennifera is native to Australia and is a terrestrial orchid. It is a type of sun orchid, characterized by its unique and colorful flowers. Within its classification, Thelymitra antennifera is a type of epigean orchid, meaning it grows above ground.
uses: Thelymitra antennifera, also known as the antenna orchid, is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Its medicinal uses include treating a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and fever. The plant's roots and leaves are typically used to prepare remedies, which are often applied topically or consumed as teas. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's effects on human health.
